edges.description: Pathogenic YWHAG dysfunction is linked to altered signaling programs that intersect with ROCK-associated cytoskeletal control in cortical neurons.
edges.target: ROCK-pathway dysregulation and maturation-dependent cytoskeletal remodeling

edges.description: Pathogenic YWHAG variants produce early-onset epilepsy as the defining clinical manifestation of DEE56.
The YWHAG seizure spectrum includes fever-triggered seizures in a substantial subset of affected individuals.
YWHAG-related epilepsy includes myoclonic seizures among the common reported seizure types.
YWHAG-related epilepsy includes absence seizures among the common reported seizure types.
YWHAG-related epilepsy includes bilateral tonic-clonic seizures among the common reported seizure types.
Some YWHAG-NDD individuals have difficult-to-control epilepsy downstream of the pathogenic variant.
Severe YWHAG-related epilepsy can progress to status epilepticus.
YWHAG dysfunction perturbs neurodevelopmental programs, producing multidomain developmental delay.
Language delay is one affected domain within the broader YWHAG neurodevelopmental phenotype.
Motor delay is one affected domain within the broader YWHAG neurodevelopmental phenotype.
YWHAG-related neurodevelopmental impairment often includes later intellectual disability.
YWHAG-related neurodevelopmental impairment includes behavioral comorbidities in many reported individuals.
Variable neurologic involvement in YWHAG syndrome can manifest with movement disorder, especially ataxia.
Altered ROCK-associated remodeling is positioned upstream of the early cytoskeletal phenotypes and the later fragility/unmasking assays.
edges.source: ROCK-pathway dysregulation and maturation-dependent cytoskeletal remodeling

edges.description: Cytoskeletal instability is associated with abnormal calcium baseline and impaired calcium homeostasis.
Cytoskeletal instability is also associated with reduced spontaneous calcium-spiking dynamics and lower network activity, consistent with partial decoupling from the baseline-calcium phenotype.
edges.target: Reduced calcium signaling and network activity

nodes.description: Absence seizures occur among the common YWHAG-NDD seizure types and can coexist with generalized tonic-clonic and myoclonic seizures.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: The most commonly reported seizure types included generalized tonic-clonic seizures, myoclonic seizures, and absence seizures.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the genotype-phenotype correlation of de novo heterozygous missense variants in YWHAG as a cause of developmental and epileptic encephalopathy.
evidence.snippet: Characteristic features included: early-onset seizures, predominantly generalized tonic-clonic and absence type (7/7) with good response to standard anti-epileptic medications

nodes.description: Antiseizure medication management is central to clinical care. Valproate has documented responsiveness in milder YWHAG myoclonic/febrile presentations, while broader YWHAG-NDD treatment response remains variable and should be individualized by epilepsy specialists.

evidence.reference_title: YWHAG Mutations Cause Childhood Myoclonic Epilepsy and Febrile Seizures: Molecular Sub-regional Effect and Mechanism.
evidence.snippet: The two probands experienced daily myoclonic seizures that were recorded with ictal generalized polyspike-slow waves, but became seizure-free with simple valproate treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Developmental and epileptic encephalopathy 56 due to YWHAG variants: 12 new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Valproic acid has emerged as an effective antiseizure medication.

nodes.description: Movement abnormalities occur in a subset of affected individuals, most often as ataxia or tremor.

evidence.reference_title: Developmental and epileptic encephalopathy 56 due to YWHAG variants: 12 new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Movement disorders were present in a subset of individuals, primarily manifesting as ataxia and tremor.

nodes.description: Behavioral comorbidities are common in YWHAG syndrome and include autism spectrum disorder, attention deficit-hyperreactivity disorder, and broader behavioral dysregulation.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and molecular characterization of patients with YWHAG-related epilepsy.
evidence.snippet: Intellectual disability (23/24, 96%), behavioral disorders (18/24, 75%), neurological signs (13/24, 54%), and dysmorphisms (6/24, 25%) are common.

evidence.reference_title: Developmental and epileptic encephalopathy 56 due to YWHAG variants: 12 new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Comorbidities such as autism spectrum disorders and attention deficit-hyperreactivity disorder were observed in a proportion of individuals.

nodes.description: Generalized or bilateral tonic-clonic seizures are among the common seizure types in YWHAG-NDD.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: The most commonly reported seizure types included generalized tonic-clonic seizures, myoclonic seizures, and absence seizures.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the genotype-phenotype correlation of de novo heterozygous missense variants in YWHAG as a cause of developmental and epileptic encephalopathy.
evidence.snippet: Characteristic features included: early-onset seizures, predominantly generalized tonic-clonic and absence type (7/7) with good response to standard anti-epileptic medications

nodes.description: YWHAG p.Arg132Cys cortical neurons show early cytoskeletal abnormalities and latent fragility that becomes more evident under Trypsin-EDTA stress. This phenotype is partially ameliorated by lovastatin in the preprint model, supporting cytoskeletal instability as a central disease-relevant mechanism rather than a secondary observation.

evidence.reference_title: Decoupled calcium homeostasis and signaling associated with cytoskeletal instability in YWHAG R132C induced pluripotent stem cell-derived cortical neurons
evidence.snippet: Exposure of YWHAG R132C/+ neurons to Trypsin-EDTA revealed underlying cytoskeletal instability, which was partially reversed by lovastatin treatment.

nodes.description: Seizures are the predominant neurologic feature of DEE56 and typically begin in infancy or early childhood, but natural-history data show that a minority of affected children may present with developmental delay without seizures. Febrile, myoclonic, absence, tonic-clonic, treatment-resistant, and status epilepticus presentations occur within the broader seizure spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Developmental and epileptic encephalopathy 56 due to YWHAG variants: 12 new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Developmental and epileptic encephalopathy 56 (DEE-56) is caused by pathogenic variants in YWHAG and is characterized by early-onset epilepsy and neurodevelopmental delay.

evidence.reference_title: Developmental and epileptic encephalopathy 56 due to YWHAG variants: 12 new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Early-onset seizures, particularly febrile seizures, were common, with various seizure types reported.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Ninety-five percent presented with seizures, while one patient presented solely with developmental delays.

nodes.description: Mutant cortical neurons exhibit an elevated calcium baseline, indicating altered calcium homeostasis. In the p.Arg132Cys model, this baseline abnormality changes with maturation, is worsened by direct ROCK inhibition with Y27632, and is partially reduced by lovastatin treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Decoupled calcium homeostasis and signaling associated with cytoskeletal instability in YWHAG R132C induced pluripotent stem cell-derived cortical neurons
evidence.snippet: Direct inhibition of ROCK with Y27632 further increased the calcium baseline compared to the isogenic control. Exposure of YWHAG R132C/+ neurons to Trypsin-EDTA revealed underlying cytoskeletal instability, which was partially reversed by lovastatin treatment. Further, lovastatin partially rescued the elevated calcium baseline, but not the frequency or amplitude of calcium spikes.

nodes.description: Fever-triggered seizures are a common seizure precipitant in YWHAG-NDD and help connect the DEE presentation with milder febrile-seizure-plus family presentations.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Sixty percent of participants reported seizures in the setting of fever.

evidence.reference_title: YWHAG Mutations Cause Childhood Myoclonic Epilepsy and Febrile Seizures: Molecular Sub-regional Effect and Mechanism.
evidence.snippet: This study suggests that YWHAG is potentially a candidate pathogenic gene of childhood myoclonic epilepsy and FS.

nodes.description: YWHAG-NDD affects multiple developmental domains, including language, cognition, social skills, and motor skills, and developmental delay may be the sole presenting feature in a minority of children.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Other notable clinical manifestations included mild to moderate delays in language skills, cognition, social skills, and motor skills.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the genotype-phenotype correlation of de novo heterozygous missense variants in YWHAG as a cause of developmental and epileptic encephalopathy.
evidence.snippet: Characteristic features included: early-onset seizures, predominantly generalized tonic-clonic and absence type (7/7) with good response to standard anti-epileptic medications; moderate DD; Intellectual Disability (ID) (5/7) and Autism Spectrum Disorder (3/7).

nodes.description: Neurodevelopmental involvement is common across the YWHAG spectrum and often includes global developmental delay with later intellectual disability. Many reported individuals have mild intellectual disability, although severity is variable.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and molecular characterization of patients with YWHAG-related epilepsy.
evidence.snippet: Intellectual disability (23/24, 96%), behavioral disorders (18/24, 75%), neurological signs (13/24, 54%), and dysmorphisms (6/24, 25%) are common.

evidence.reference_title: Developmental and epileptic encephalopathy 56 due to YWHAG variants: 12 new cases and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Individuals with YWHAG variants exhibited variable psychomotor delay, with the majority experiencing mild intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Variants such as p.Leu173Ser may impair YWHAG function by destabilizing the conserved internal hydrophobic core rather than primarily affecting the ligand-binding groove.
Missense variants in the phosphopeptide ligand-binding region are enriched among patients with the more severe DEE presentation.
Motor-skill delay is part of the multidomain developmental impairment reported in YWHAG-NDD natural-history data.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Other notable clinical manifestations included mild to moderate delays in language skills, cognition, social skills, and motor skills.

nodes.description: Myoclonic seizures are among the most commonly reported YWHAG-NDD seizure types and are prominent in milder familial febrile-seizure-plus presentations.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: The most commonly reported seizure types included generalized tonic-clonic seizures, myoclonic seizures, and absence seizures.

evidence.reference_title: YWHAG Mutations Cause Childhood Myoclonic Epilepsy and Febrile Seizures: Molecular Sub-regional Effect and Mechanism.
evidence.snippet: The two probands experienced daily myoclonic seizures that were recorded with ictal generalized polyspike-slow waves, but became seizure-free with simple valproate treatment.

nodes.description: Occupational therapy and adaptive supports should be considered for functional, social, and daily-living needs related to multidomain developmental impairment.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Adaptive function was assessed using the Vineland-3 Adaptive Behavior Scales.

nodes.description: Physical therapy can support motor-skill acquisition, mobility, balance, and functional participation when motor delay or coordination problems are present.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Other notable clinical manifestations included mild to moderate delays in language skills, cognition, social skills, and motor skills.

nodes.description: In YWHAG p.Arg132Cys cortical neurons, transcriptomic dysregulation implicates the ROCK pathway and associated modulators, with biphasic maturation-dependent changes in cytoskeletal protein stability and calcium phenotypes. This node is kept distinct from the overt cytoskeletal fragility phenotype because the study suggests a signaling/remodeling layer upstream of the physical instability phenotype.
nodes.id: ROCK-pathway dysregulation and maturation-dependent cytoskeletal remodeling

evidence.reference_title: Decoupled calcium homeostasis and signaling associated with cytoskeletal instability in YWHAG R132C induced pluripotent stem cell-derived cortical neurons
evidence.snippet: The widespread alterations in the transcriptome of mutant neurons revealed a biphasic dysregulation in the core genes and modulators associated with the ROCK pathway that resulted in maturation-dependent changes to cytoskeletal protein stability and calcium phenotypes.

nodes.description: Despite the elevated baseline calcium phenotype, YWHAG p.Arg132Cys cortical neurons have lower-frequency calcium spikes and reduced network activity. Lovastatin does not rescue calcium spike frequency or amplitude, supporting a partial decoupling between calcium homeostasis and activity-dependent calcium signaling in mutant neurons.
nodes.id: Reduced calcium signaling and network activity

evidence.reference_title: Decoupled calcium homeostasis and signaling associated with cytoskeletal instability in YWHAG R132C induced pluripotent stem cell-derived cortical neurons
evidence.snippet: The YWHAG R132C/+ iPSC-derived neurons exhibited early cytoskeletal phenotypes, coupled with an elevated calcium baseline, lower frequency of calcium spikes, and reduced network activity.

evidence.reference_title: Decoupled calcium homeostasis and signaling associated with cytoskeletal instability in YWHAG R132C induced pluripotent stem cell-derived cortical neurons
evidence.snippet: Further, lovastatin partially rescued the elevated calcium baseline, but not the frequency or amplitude of calcium spikes.

nodes.description: Language delay is part of the multidomain developmental impairment in YWHAG-NDD and should be evaluated separately from later intellectual disability.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Other notable clinical manifestations included mild to moderate delays in language skills, cognition, social skills, and motor skills.

nodes.description: Speech-language therapy should address language delay and communication needs in children with YWHAG-NDD.

evidence.snippet: Other notable clinical manifestations included mild to moderate delays in language skills, cognition, social skills, and motor skills.

nodes.description: Because status epilepticus is reported in a substantial subset of YWHAG-NDD, care plans should include emergency seizure instructions and access to acute rescue pharmacotherapy according to local epilepsy protocols.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Fifty-five percent of participants reported treatment-resistant epilepsy and 40% reported at least one episode of status epilepticus.

nodes.description: Status epilepticus is a recurrent severe seizure outcome in YWHAG-NDD and should drive seizure action planning and emergency care preparation.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Fifty-five percent of participants reported treatment-resistant epilepsy and 40% reported at least one episode of status epilepticus.

nodes.description: More than half of participants in the prospective natural-history cohort had treatment-resistant epilepsy, indicating that seizure management can be difficult despite valproate-responsive milder presentations in some families.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of YWHAG-Associated Neurodevelopmental Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Fifty-five percent of participants reported treatment-resistant epilepsy and 40% reported at least one episode of status epilepticus.

nodes.description: Truncating YWHAG variants can segregate with a milder familial phenotype dominated by febrile seizures or myoclonic epilepsy, consistent with haploinsufficiency being less severe than many ligand-binding-domain missense alleles.

YWHAG encodes the 14-3-3gamma scaffold protein, which coordinates phosphoserine-dependent signaling complexes important for neuronal development. Pathogenic missense variants can perturb the protein through at least two partially distinct mechanisms: recurrent ligand-binding-domain alleles associated with more severe DEE and structural-destabilizing alleles that disrupt the conserved internal hydrophobic core. The recurrent p.Arg132Cys ligand-binding-domain variant provides the best-studied bridge from human genetics to downstream neuronal mechanism.

evidence.reference_title: De Novo Mutations in YWHAG Cause Early-Onset Epilepsy.
evidence.snippet: We identified a highly significant enrichment of de novo variants in YWHAG, establishing their role in early-onset epilepsy

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and molecular characterization of patients with YWHAG-related epilepsy.
evidence.snippet: A genotype-phenotype correlation emerged, as DEE is more represented in patients with missense variants located in the ligand-binding domain than in those with truncating or missense variants in other domains

evidence.reference_title: A Novel YWHAG Variant L173S Causes Developmental and Epileptic Encephalopathy by Disrupting the Hydrophobic Internal Protein Structure.
evidence.snippet: Our findings suggest that the disruption of the stability of the highly conserved internal core of the YWHAG protein may be one mechanism leading to functional impairment, distinct from the previously proposed pathogenic models of dimer formation defects and/or impaired binding to phosphopeptide ligands.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and molecular characterization of patients with YWHAG-related epilepsy.

evidence.reference_title: YWHAG Mutations Cause Childhood Myoclonic Epilepsy and Febrile Seizures: Molecular Sub-regional Effect and Mechanism.
evidence.snippet: The truncating mutation was identified in the family with six individuals of mild phenotype, suggesting that YWHAG mutations of haploinsufficiency are relatively less pathogenic.

evidence.reference_title: A Novel YWHAG Variant L173S Causes Developmental and Epileptic Encephalopathy by Disrupting the Hydrophobic Internal Protein Structure.
